ucs.title: [LLM] Cyber Av3ngers (IRGC-CEC) — Unitronics Vision PLC PCOM exposure & default-credential abuse on TCP/20256
ucs.description: Hunts inbound or unsanctioned connections to Unitronics Vision/Samba series PLC programming port (TCP/20256, PCOM protocol). This is the signature initial-access vector of Cyber Av3ngers (CISA AA23-335A): they scan the internet for Israeli-made Unitronics PLCs reachable from the public internet, log in with vendor default password '1111', and overwrite HMI logic/screens with their 'Down with Israel' defacement.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true allow_old_summaries=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.action) as action values(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as bytes_in values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where All_Traffic.dest_port=20256 OR All_Traffic.src_port=20256 by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.src_category All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.dest_category All_Traffic.dest_port All_Traffic.transport All_Traffic.app | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)` | eval is_external_src=if(cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",src) OR cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",src) OR cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",src),0,1) | eval is_engineering_app=if(match(app,"(?i)unilogic|visilogic|remote.?operator|pcom"),1,0) | where is_external_src=1 OR is_engineering_app=0 | eval threat_actor="Cyber Av3ngers / IRGC-CEC", note="TCP/20256 = Unitronics PCOM. Validate against engineering jump-host allowlist; investigate any external src." | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | sort - count
ucs.kql:
let UnitronicsPort = 20256;
let EngineeringTools = dynamic(["unilogic.exe","visilogic.exe","remoteoperator.exe","u90ladder.exe"]);
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where RemotePort == UnitronicsPort or LocalPort == UnitronicsPort
| where ActionType in ("ConnectionSuccess","InboundConnectionAccepted","ConnectionAttempt","ConnectionFailed")
| extend RemoteIsPublic = not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
| extend ToolUsed = tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName)
| extend IsSanctionedTool = ToolUsed in (EngineeringTools)
| where RemoteIsPublic == true or IsSanctionedTool == false
| summarize ConnAttempts=count(), FirstSeen=min(Timestamp), LastSeen=max(Timestamp), Apps=make_set(InitiatingProcessFileName,10), Cmds=make_set(InitiatingProcessCommandLine,5) by DeviceName, LocalIP, RemoteIP, RemotePort, ActionType, RemoteIsPublic
| extend ThreatActor="Cyber Av3ngers (IRGC-CEC, Iran)", Reference="CISA AA23-335A", Signature="Unitronics PCOM exposure / default-cred abuse"
| order by ConnAttempts desc
ucs.rationale: Port 20256 is the Unitronics PCOM (Programming Communication) port — there is essentially zero legitimate reason for it to traverse the IT corporate boundary or be reached from arbitrary endpoints. Cyber Av3ngers' November-2023 Aliquippa/PA water-authority and follow-on campaigns are uniquely defined by mass-scanning this port and using vendor default password '1111'. A generic 'expose-public-PLC'

ucs.title: [LLM] Cyber Av3ngers — engineering-workstation pivot: VisiLogic/UniLogic spawning PowerShell or downloading PLC project payloads
ucs.description: Hunts the IT→OT pivot stage where Cyber Av3ngers compromises an engineering workstation (via T1566 phishing or T1190 exposed VPN/Fortinet) and then abuses the legitimate Unitronics IDE binaries to push tampered ladder logic or HMI defacement images to PLCs. Detects anomalous child processes / network egress from VisiLogic/UniLogic that indicate hijacked engineering tooling rather than normal commissioning.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process) as parent values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.parent_process_name IN ("VisiLogic.exe","UniLogic.exe","RemoteOperator.exe","U90Ladder.exe")) AND Processes.process_name IN ("powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | eval threat_actor="Cyber Av3ngers / IRGC-CEC", stage="Engineering workstation hijack -> PLC tampering pivot" | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let EngineeringIDEs = dynamic(["visilogic.exe","unilogic.exe","remoteoperator.exe","u90ladder.exe"]);
let LolBins = dynamic(["powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","certutil.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","msiexec.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(14d)
| where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (EngineeringIDEs)
| where tolower(FileName) in (LolBins)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, ProcessId
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(14d)
    | where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (EngineeringIDEs)
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
    | summarize ExternalDest=make_set(strcat(RemoteIP,":",tostring(RemotePort)),20) by DeviceName
) on DeviceName
| extend ThreatActor="Cyber Av3ngers (IRGC-CEC)", Reference="CISA AA23-335A; Microsoft Storm-0784 / Pink Sandstorm overlap", Signature="Hijacked Unitronics IDE spawning LOLBin or beaconing externally"
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Cyber Av3ngers does not write novel malware — they weaponize the legitimate vendor IDE (VisiLogic/UniLogic) to push ladder-logic/HMI changes after gaining footholds via Iranian-pattern phishing (T1566) or exposed remote services (T1133). Generic 'Office spawning PowerShell' rules miss this entirely; pivoting on `VisiLogic.exe`/`UniLogic.exe` as the parent of LOLBins (or their unexpected outbound t
